risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "They were randomly assigned to one of the three treatments: high dairy group, high calcium group, and control." Comment: The study states that participants were randomly assigned, but it does not describe the sequence-generation method (e.g., computer-generated numbers, random number table, blocks, or other explicit random procedure). Under Cochrane guidance, "randomized" alone without additional context is insufficient to confirm a truly random sequence, so the risk remains unclear.

_evidence.method_quote: Thirty subjects were enrolled in the study. They were randomly assigned to one of the three treatments: high dairy group, high calcium group, and control.
_evidence.method_summary: Participants were randomly assigned to one of three treatment groups; no sequence-generation method is described.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The article states that subjects were "randomly assigned" to the three treatments, but it does not describe any method of allocation concealment (e.g., central allocation, sequentially numbered opaque sealed envelopes, or identical containers). Comment: Because the recruiter’s ability to foresee the next assignment is not addressed, this domain remains unclear by Cochrane criteria.

_evidence.note: The article states that subjects were "randomly assigned" but provides no details on how the assignment sequence was concealed from recruiters.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "single-center, randomized, double-blind (single-blinded for the dairy group), placebo-controlled trial" and "subjects in the control group were instructed to consume their usual diet plus a placebo tablet". Comment: The study explicitly reports blinding of the trial and use of placebo control; although blinding was incomplete for the dairy group, the main outcomes were objective measurements (body weight, BMI, DXA-derived body composition, and serum lipids), which are unlikely to be materially influenced by participants’ or personnel’s knowledge of allocation.

_evidence.blinding_quote: single-center, randomized, double-blind (single-blinded for the dairy group), placebo-controlled trial
_evidence.blinding_method: placebo-controlled trial; subjects in the control group were instructed to consume their usual diet plus a placebo tablet; the trial was described as "double-blind (single-blinded for the dairy group)"

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Body weight was measured monthly with a calibrated scale... by the CRC-trained personnel, who were blinded to the study treatments. Lean body mass, total and trunk fat mass, and total and trunk percentage fat were assessed... by dual energy x-ray abosorptiometry... Cholesterol ... and triacylglycerol levels were determined by a colorimetric assay using a commercial kit." Comment: The outcome assessors for the key measured outcomes were blinded, and the outcomes reported here are objective measurements (weight, DXA-derived body composition, and laboratory assays). Knowledge of allocation is unlikely to have influenced outcome measurement.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote:
Body weight was measured monthly with a calibrated scale (Detecto Inc., Northbrook, IL, USA). Height was measured at baseline using a wall-mounted tape. Measurements were taken while subjects wore light clothing, no shoes or accessories, by the CRC-trained personnel, who were blinded to the study treatments.
Lean body mass, total and trunk fat mass, and total and trunk percentage fat were assessed at baseline and at 21 wk by dual energy x-ray abosorptiometry (Hologic DP-W; Hologic, Bedford, MA, USA). Total bone mineral content (TBMC) and total bone mineral density (TBMD) were also assessed from dual energy x-ray abosorptiometry measurements.
Blood samples were collected at baseline and at 21 wk, between 0700 and 1100 after a 12-h fast. Then they were immediately centrifuged at 12 000 × g for 10 min, and serum was stored at −80°C. Cholesterol (total, low-density lipoprotein (LDL), and high-density lipoprotein (HDL)) and triacylglycerol levels were determined by a colorimetric assay using a commercial kit (Vitros Chemical Products, Ortho Clinical Diagnostics, Raritan, NJ, USA).

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Five subjects dropped out from the study before the second visit; four females and one male. The reasons for not continuing in the study were as follows: one had medical reasons and four lost interest." and Table 1 shows "High dairy group (n = 8)", "High calcium group (n = 9)", "Control group (n = 8)" with Table 2 analysing the same n values at 21 wk. Comment: The primary analysed outcome appears to have complete follow-up among the 25 completers (8/9/8 analysed out of 8/9/8 randomised per arm), with 0% post-randomization loss at the analysed time point and no meaningful arm imbalance; reasons for the 5 dropouts were mostly loss of interest and one medical reason, but these losses occurred before the final analysis and do not indicate biased incomplete outcome data.

_evidence.notes: 5 subjects dropped out before the second visit, but the table and analysed results indicate per-arm n=8/9/8 with the 25 completers analysed. The study also reports a sensitivity analysis on only compliant subjects (high dairy n=7; high calcium n=9; control n=6), but the primary analysed outcome appears to use all completers.
